prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Yer want ta know why the sky be blue, eh? Well, matey, I be thinkin' it's because o' the way the sun's rays be scatterin' about. Ye see, when the sun shines, it sends out all sorts o' colors, like a great ol' rainbow, but mostly blue, like the depths o' the ocean.

Now, I be thinkin' that the blue be scatterin' more than the other colors 'cause it be shorter, like a wee little scallywag darting about. So it bounces off all the tiny particles in the air, like the sea spray on a windy day. That be why the sky appears blue, me hearty!

Some o' them landlubbers back in England be sayin' it's because o' some fancy thing called Rayleigh scatterin', but I be thinkin' that's just a bunch o' bilge water. Me explanation be good enough fer me, and I be stickin' to it!

So hoist the sails and set course fer a day o' gazing at the blue sky, me hearties! And may the winds o' fortune be at yer back! Arrr!
